structure.title: Preface
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is an E-Tender notice issued by Indian Railway Catering & Tourism Corporation Limited (IRCTC) for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP). The main points covered include:

## Key Tender Details:
- **Purpose**: Selection of TSP for Development, Deployment, Operation, upgrading and Ad Monetization of AI-based Chatbot Solutions (ASKDISHA) for IRCTC Services
- **Tender Type**: Open E-Tender with QCBS (Quality and Cost Based Selection) Two-Packet system (Technical and Financial Bids)
- **Important Dates**: 
  - Submission deadline: July 24, 2025 at 1500 Hrs
  - Technical bid opening: July 24, 2025 at 1530 Hrs
  - Pre-bid queries deadline: July 08, 2025 at 1500 Hrs
  - Pre-bid meeting: July 10, 2025 at 12:30 Hrs (online)
- **Earnest Money Deposit**: ₹5,00,000
- **Bid Validity**: 120 days from bid opening date

## Main Sections Covered:
1. **General Information**: About IRCTC and the chatbot ASKDISHA
2. **Objectives**: Goals and purpose of the tender
3. **Scope of Work**: Detailed requirements for chatbot development and deployment
4. **Key Deliverables**: Expected outputs from both bidder and IRCTC
5. **Contract Terms**: Period and conditions of engagement
6. **Consortium Approach**: Guidelines for joint bidding
7. **Evaluation Criteria**: 
   - Eligibility requirements
   - Technical scoring methodology
   - Financial bid evaluation
   - QCBS evaluation process
8. **Payment Terms**: Financial arrangements and schedules
9. **SLA & Penalties**: Service level agreements and penalty clauses
10. **Intellectual Property Rights**: Ownership and usage rights of developed solutions
11. **Disclaimer**: Legal protections and reservations by IRCTC

The document emphasizes IRCTC's rights to modify, reject, or withdraw the tender at any stage without liability.

structure.title: Contents
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is a **Table of Contents and Notice Inviting Tender (NIT)** for an e-tender issued by the Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Limited (IRCTC). The main points covered include:

## Primary Purpose:
Selection of a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for the development, deployment, operation, upgrading, and ad monetization of AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services.

## Key Sections Outlined:

1. **General Information**: About the AskDisha chatbot, objectives, and scope of work

2. **Technical Requirements**: 
   - Key deliverables and benchmarks
   - Consortium approach options
   - Service Level Agreements (SLA) and penalty clauses

3. **Bid Evaluation Process**:
   - Two-packet system (Technical and Financial bids)
   - Eligibility and technical scoring criteria
   - Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) of Rs. 5,00,000
   - Quality and Cost-Based Selection (QCBS) methodology

4. **Contract Terms**:
   - Contract period of 3 years
   - Payment terms
   - Intellectual Property Rights (IPR)
   - Termination and exit clauses

5. **Legal and Compliance**:
   - Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)
   - Integrity Pact
   - Information security and audit requirements
   - Arbitration and Force Majeure provisions

6. **Annexures**: Various forms, undertakings, and checklists including blocked ad categories, cloud storage requirements, and document checklists

The bid validity period is 120 days from the tender opening date, with IRCTC reserving the right to reject any or all bids.

structure.title: SECTION -I: NOTICE INVITING TENDER
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is a tender notice from Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Limited (IRCTC) for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP). The main points covered include:

## Key Components:

1. **Tender Purpose**: Selection of TSP for development, deployment, operation, upgrading, and ad monetization of AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services

2. **Contract Duration**: Three (3) years

3. **Financial Requirements**:
   - Earnest Money Deposit (EMD): Rs 5,00,000 (Five Lakhs Only)
   - Mandatory requirement; offers without EMD will be rejected

4. **Bid Structure**:
   - Two-packet system: Technical Bid and Financial Bid
   - Technical Bid covers qualification and conditions
   - Financial Bid covers financial aspects

5. **Bid Validity**: 120 days from the date of tender opening (extendable on request)

6. **Submission Process**:
   - E-tender only (no physical offers accepted)
   - Submission through https://www.tenderwizard.com/IRCTC
   - Mandatory registration on Tender Wizard platform
   - Class-III Digital Signature required
   - Documents viewable at www.irctc.com and tenderwizard.com/IRCTC

7. **Important Conditions**:
   - IRCTC reserves right to reject any/all tenders without reason
   - "No Deviation Bid" tender
   - Corrigendum/addendum published online only
   - Pre-bid meeting available for clarifications

8. **Document Components**: Notice Inviting Tender (NIT), Instructions to Bidders, Special Conditions of Contract (SCC), E-Tender Schedule, and General Conditions of Contract (GCC)

structure.title: SECTION – II: INSTRUCTIONS TO BIDDER
structure.summary:
This partial document describes the instructions to bidders for an e-tender issued by Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Limited (IRCTC) for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP). The main points covered include:

1. **Project Scope**: Development, deployment, operation, upgrading, and ad monetization of AI-based chatbot and WhatsApp solutions for IRCTC services.

2. **E-Tender Process Requirements**:
   - Tender documents available only online at IRCTC and TenderWizard websites
   - No physical offers accepted
   - Mandatory online registration on TenderWizard platform
   - Class-III Digital Signature required for bid submission

3. **Key Instructions**:
   - Corrigendum/addendum will be published online only, no newspaper advertisements
   - Bidders must upload complete set of required documents
   - Helpdesk contact information provided for technical difficulties
   - Digital signature confirms acceptance of all tender terms and conditions

4. **Bidder Obligations**:
   - Study and understand all tender conditions
   - Consider overall project scope while preparing offers
   - Seek clarifications during pre-bid meeting if needed

5. **Bid Specifications**:
   - Bid validity: 120 days from last submission date
   - "No Deviation Bid" tender type

structure.title: GENERAL
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document outlines an e-tender issued by the Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Limited (IRCTC) for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP). The main points covered include:

## Key Components:

1. **Tender Purpose**: Selection of TSP for development, deployment, operation, upgrading, and ad monetization of AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services.

2. **General Instructions to Bidders**:
   - E-tender accessible only through specified websites (irctc.com and tenderwizard.com/IRCTC)
   - No physical offers accepted; online submission only
   - Mandatory registration on Tender Wizard platform
   - Class-III Digital Signature required
   - Bid validity: 120 days from submission deadline
   - Designated as "No Deviation Bid"

3. **About AskDisha Chatbot**: 
   - IRCTC's existing AI-powered chatbot for real-time railway-related assistance
   - Part of digital customer engagement enhancement initiative

4. **Tender Objectives**:
   - Implement conversational voice and text-based solution (Chatbot and WhatsApp Bot)
   - Enable end-to-end customer support and ticket booking
   - Provide multi-language conversational capabilities with regional language support
   - Utilize advanced technologies including Generative AI and Agentic AI
   - Create revenue-generating platform through in-chat advertisements and service charges

5. **Document Structure**: References to Special Conditions of Contract (SCC) and General Conditions of Contract (GCC) that govern the agreement.

structure.title: ABOUT CHATBOT ASKDISHA
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is an E-Tender notice from the Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Ltd. (IRCTC) for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP). The main points covered include:

1. **Document Structure**: The document outlines Special Conditions of Contract (SCC) that override General Conditions of Contract (GCC), covering scope of work, bidder instructions, and contractual terms.

2. **Background on AskDisha Chatbot**: IRCTC has an existing AI-powered chatbot called AskDisha (Digital Interaction to Seek Help Anytime) that provides real-time assistance for railway-related queries as part of their digital customer engagement initiative.

3. **Purpose of Tender**: IRCTC seeks to engage a qualified TSP to develop, deploy, operate, upgrade, and monetize an advanced AI-based chatbot solution for enhanced digital service delivery.

4. **Key Objectives**:
   - Implement conversational voice and text-based solutions (Chatbot and WhatsApp Bot) for end-to-end customer support and ticket booking on IRCTC's website and mobile app
   - Provide multi-language conversational capabilities with high accuracy and regional language support
   - Enhance user experience using advanced technologies like Generative AI and Agentic AI for intelligent, human-like interactions
   - Develop a revenue-generating platform through in-chat advertisements and service charges

5. **Technical Requirements**: The solution must support multiple digital channels, offer personalized multilingual experiences, and integrate seamlessly with IRCTC's existing platforms.

structure.title: OBJECTIVES OF THE TENDER
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is an e-tender specification for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop and operate an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC (Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Ltd.).

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Document Structure**: The document outlines Special Conditions of Contract (SCC) that override General Conditions of Contract (GCC), covering scope of work, bidder instructions, and special conditions.

2. **Background - AskDisha Chatbot**: Introduction to IRCTC's existing AI-powered chatbot "AskDisha" (Digital Interaction to Seek Help Anytime) that provides real-time assistance for railway-related queries.

3. **Tender Objectives**: 
   - Implement voice and text-based conversational solutions (Chatbot and WhatsApp Bot)
   - Provide multi-language support with high accuracy
   - Utilize advanced technologies like Generative AI and Agentic AI
   - Generate revenue through in-chat advertisements
   - Ensure data privacy and cybersecurity compliance with DPDP guidelines
   - Enable white-label solution for third-party clients
   - Ensure smooth knowledge transfer and migration at contract end

4. **Scope of Work**:
   - Design, develop, deploy, and maintain chatbot/WhatsApp bot for IRCTC's platforms (e-ticketing website, Rail Connect app, Travel/tourism platforms)
   - **Booking & Enquiry Services**: Train ticket booking, PNR status, fare enquiry, seat availability, train schedules, live train status, and cluster station information
   - **Multi-Modal Interaction**: Support for both voice-based (STT/TTS) and text-based interactions across web and mobile platforms
   - **Multilingual Capability**: (section begins but details not included in this excerpt)

structure.title: SCOPE OF WORK
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document: IRCTC AI-Based Chatbot Solutions E-Tender

## Main Points Covered:

### 1. **Project Objectives**
- Ensure robust data privacy and cybersecurity compliance with DPDP guidelines
- Enable IRCTC to offer chatbot/WhatsApp bot as a service to third-party clients
- Ensure smooth knowledge transfer and migration at contract end

### 2. **Scope of Work**
- Design, develop, deploy, and maintain AI-based chatbot/WhatsApp bot for IRCTC's business verticals (e-ticketing, Rail Connect app, Travel/Tourism platforms)
- Support booking and enquiry services including PNR status, fare enquiry, seat availability, train schedules, and live running status

### 3. **Technical Capabilities Required**
- **Multi-modal interaction**: Voice and text-based (STT/TTS technologies)
- **Multilingual support**: 12 Indian languages (English, Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Bengali, Marathi, Gujarati, Malayalam, Punjabi, Odia, Assamese)
- **Omnichannel deployment**: Website, mobile app, and WhatsApp with consistent user experience
- **Conversational AI**: Handle imprecise queries, maintain context, adapt to user behavior
- **Advanced AI Integration**: Generative AI and Agentic AI capabilities

### 4. **AI Features**
- Natural conversations and dynamic query handling
- Content personalization and context memory
- Proactive assistance and goal-oriented task execution
- Intelligent cross-selling (meals, cabs, hotels)
- Video bot assistance capabilities
- Custom itinerary generation combining multiple services

### 5. **WhatsApp Bot Requirements**
- Procurement of memorable mobile number for verified WhatsApp Business API
- Mobile number remains IRCTC property
- Obtain approvals from Meta, TRAI for message templates

### 6. **Advertisement and Monetization**
- Non-intrusive, contextually relevant digital advertisements
- Text or banner-based ads tied to user queries
- Ad placement within chatbot/WhatsApp flows
- Blocked categories enforcement
- Revenue sharing model: 70% IRCTC, 30% Bidder

### 7. **Revenue Model**
- **Service Fee**: ₹10 per chatbot/WhatsApp-initiated ticket booking
- **Revenue Sharing**: 70% IRCTC, 30% TSP (subject to minimum committed amount)
- Total revenue includes ticket booking fees plus advertisement revenue
- Integrated dashboard for transparent revenue tracking

### 8. **Value-Added Services**
- Integration of air booking, tourism services, hotels, flights, buses, tour packages
- Premium e-catering services promotion
- Cross-selling and upselling capabilities
- Co-branded co-sell model for government and private organizations

### 9. **IT Security and Compliance**
- All customer data remains exclusive property of IRCTC
- No external use of customer data for model training or marketing
- Data hosting within India
- Compliance with CERT-IN Guidelines, DPDP Act 2023, IRCTC security policies
- Full data deletion post-contract

### 10. **Reporting and Monitoring**
Required reports include:
- Booking summary reports
- Advertisement performance reports
- Language-wise interaction reports
- SLA compliance reports
- Data privacy and security audit reports

### 11. **Key Deliverables**
- Fully functional IRCTC-branded AI chatbot/WhatsApp bot
- End-to-end API integration with IRCTC backend
- No license, software, hardware, or infrastructure costs to IRCTC
- Multilingual conversational capabilities
- Ad monetization platform with real-time analytics
- SMS/Email communication management
- Support for various ad formats (Banner, GIF, Display, Video, Native)
- Downloadable reports in Excel/CSV formats

structure.title: KEY DELIVERABLES AND BENCHMARKS BY THE BIDDER
structure.summary:
This partial document outlines the requirements and deliverables for an e-tender to select a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing and deploying an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **Reporting Requirements**: Mandatory submission of various reports including booking summaries, advertisement performance metrics, language-wise interaction data, SLA compliance reports, and data privacy/security audit reports.

2. **Key Deliverables by the Bidder**: 
   - Development and deployment of IRCTC-branded AI chatbot across multiple channels
   - Complete API integration with IRCTC backend systems
   - Free integration of IRCTC e-ticketing application with no licensing or infrastructure costs to IRCTC
   - Multilingual support capabilities
   - Ad monetization platform with analytics dashboard
   - Data security and privacy compliance documentation
   - Management of SMS/email communications
   - Support for multiple advertisement formats
   - Provision of downloadable reports and escalation matrix

3. **Key Deliverables by IRCTC**:
   - Provision of Web Service APIs for e-ticketing integration
   - Handling of customer complaints and queries
   - Provision of FAQs and railway information
   - Promotional support at IRCTC's discretion

4. **Contract Terms**:
   - Initial contract period of 3 years with potential extension of up to 2 additional years
   - Letter of Award (LOA) process with 15-day acceptance timeline
   - Requirement for agreement and NDA signing
   - IRCTC's right to conduct audits and control logo usage

structure.title: KEY DELIVERABLES BY THE IRCTC
structure.summary:
This partial document describes key contractual terms and deliverables for an e-tender to select a Technology Service Provider for developing an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC railway services. The main points covered include:

1. **General Contract Conditions**: Requirements for service level agreements, NDA signing, IRCTC's audit rights, restrictions on logo usage, and escalation matrix provision.

2. **IRCTC's Deliverables**: 
   - Provision of Web Service APIs for e-ticketing integration
   - Handling of customer complaints and queries
   - Promotion of AI-based ticket booking through chatbot
   - Supply of FAQs and railway information for chatbot integration

3. **Contract Period Terms**:
   - Initial tenure of 3 years with possible extensions of up to 2 additional years (1+1 year)
   - Letter of Award (LOA) issuance process
   - 15-day acceptance timeline for LOA
   - Requirement for subsequent agreement and NDA signing
   - Extension based on satisfactory performance and revenue realization at IRCTC's discretion

structure.title: CONTRACT PERIOD TERMS AND CONDITIONS
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document outlines key sections of an e-tender for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop and operate an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

## 1. General Terms and Conditions
- Service level agreement and NDA requirements with the successful bidder
- IRCTC's right to conduct audits for business transparency
- Restrictions on using IRCTC's logo without prior approval
- Requirement for bidder to provide escalation matrix for issue handling

## 2. IRCTC's Key Deliverables
- Provision of Web Service APIs for e-ticketing integration
- Support for chatbot accessibility via IRCTC website and mobile apps (Android & iOS)
- Handling of customer complaints and queries
- Provision of FAQs and railway-related information from authentic sources
- Potential promotion of AI-based ticket booking through chatbot solution

## 3. Contract Period Terms
- Initial contract duration of 3 years
- Possible extension of up to 2 additional years (1+1 year) based on performance
- Letter of Award (LOA) issuance process with 15-day acceptance timeline
- Requirement for subsequent agreement and NDA signing

## 4. Consortium Approach Guidelines
- Allowance for consortium bidding with maximum two members
- Lead member must hold minimum 51% stake in contract value
- Pre-qualification criteria requirements for consortiums
- Lead member responsibilities as primary point of contact
- Power of Attorney and joint bidding agreement requirements
- Restrictions on multiple consortium participation
- Sub-contracting limitations (except for cloud service providers)
- Special provisions for MSMEs/Start-ups

structure.title: CONSORTIUM APPROACH
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document outlines the **Consortium Approach and Bid Evaluation guidelines** for an e-tender related to selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services.

## Main Points Covered:

### 1. **Consortium Formation Rules**
- Companies can form consortiums to bid, given the diverse skills required
- Maximum of two (2) consortium members allowed, including the lead member
- Lead member must hold minimum 51% stake in contract value throughout the contract period

### 2. **Lead Member Responsibilities**
- Serves as primary point of contact with IRCTC
- Holds primary responsibility for contract obligations
- All consortium members are jointly and individually responsible for successful execution

### 3. **Documentation Requirements**
- Power of Attorney from consortium members favoring the lead member
- Joint bidding agreement defining roles, scope, and responsibilities of each member
- Clear delineation of each member's relationship and work scope

### 4. **Restrictions and Disqualifications**
- No bidder can participate in multiple consortiums for the same RFP
- Sub-contracting prohibited except for cloud service providers
- MSMEs/Start-ups using turnover relaxation benefits cannot form consortiums

### 5. **Conflict of Interest Provisions**
- Detailed criteria for identifying conflicts of interest including common ownership, shared constituents, financial relationships, common legal representation, and prior consultancy roles

### 6. **Bid Evaluation System**
- Quality cum Cost Based (QCBS) Two Packet Tender system

structure.title: EVALUATION OF BIDS

nodes.title: One or Two Packet System
nodes.summary:
This partial document covers the following main points:

**Description of Partial Document:**

This section of the tender document for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for AI-based Chatbot Solutions for IRCTC Services addresses:

1. **Consortium and MSME/Start-up Provisions**: Clarifies that MSMEs/Start-ups availing turnover criteria relaxation cannot use consortium approach provisions.

2. **Conflict of Interest Guidelines**: Establishes comprehensive rules to prevent conflicts of interest among bidders, including restrictions on:
   - Common ownership or shareholders between bidders
   - Shared constituents among bidders
   - Financial relationships between bidders
   - Shared legal representation
   - Relationships that could influence bidding
   - Prior consultant involvement in project preparation

3. **Bid Evaluation Framework**: Outlines the evaluation process including:
   - Two-packet Quality cum Cost Based (QCBS) tender system
   - Required bid contents and documentation
   - Online submission requirement

4. **Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) Requirements**: Specifies:
   - EMD amount of Rs. 5,00,000
   - Validity period of 90 days
   - No interest payment
   - Forfeiture conditions
   - Return conditions for successful and unsuccessful bidders

5. **Administrative Requirements**: Covers language requirements (English) and establishment of Tender Evaluation Committee (TEC) for bid assessment.

nodes.title: Contents of the Bid
nodes.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This partial document outlines the bidding requirements and evaluation process for an e-tender seeking a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop, deploy, operate, upgrade, and monetize an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services. 

**Main Points Covered:**

1. **Bid Contents Requirements (Section 8.2):**
   - Bidders must consider overall project schedule when preparing offers
   - Required components include: bid security declaration compliance, eligibility criteria, technical evaluation information, financial bid in specified formats, and other relevant information
   - Only online bid submissions accepted; offline bids will be rejected

2. **Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) Requirements (Section 8.3):**
   - Mandatory EMD amount: Rs. 5,00,000 (Five Lakhs)
   - Non-submission results in summary rejection
   - EMD validity: 90 days from tender opening (extendable)
   - No interest payable on EMD
   - EMD forfeiture conditions for bid withdrawal or amendments
   - Return policy: successful bidders receive EMD back after Security Deposit submission; unsuccessful bidders after tender finalization

3. **Language Requirements (Section 8.4):**
   - All proposals, correspondence, and documents must be in English

4. **Tender Evaluation Process (Section 8.5):**
   - Formation of Tender Evaluation Committee (TEC) by IRCTC
   - Committee evaluates bidder responses and supporting documentation
   - Lack of requisite supporting documents may lead to rejection
   - Multi-stage evaluation process (stages not fully detailed in this excerpt)

nodes.title: Tender Evaluation Process
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document outlines the bidding process and requirements for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop, deploy, operate, and upgrade an AI-based chatbot solution with ad monetization capabilities for IRCTC Services. The main points covered include:

## Key Components:

1. **Bid Contents and Submission Requirements**
   - Bidders must provide information for bid security declaration, eligibility criteria, and technical evaluation
   - Financial bid submission in specified formats
   - Only online bid submissions accepted; offline bids will be rejected

2. **Earnest Money Deposit (EMD)**
   - Required amount: Rs. 5,00,000 (Five Lakhs)
   - Non-submission leads to bid rejection
   - Valid for 90 days from tender opening date
   - No interest payable on EMD
   - Forfeiture conditions and return process outlined

3. **Tender Evaluation Process**
   - Three-stage evaluation process:
     - Stage 1: Eligibility qualification and technical scoring (minimum 70 marks required on QCBS basis)
     - Stage 2: Financial bid opening for qualified bidders
     - Stage 3: Combined technical and financial evaluation using QCBS methodology
   - Tender Evaluation Committee (TEC) to be constituted
   - Committee decisions are final
   - Clarification process during evaluation permitted without price changes

4. **General Requirements**
   - All proposals and correspondence must be in English
   - Bidders must consider overall project schedule
   - Incomplete bids may be rejected as unresponsive

5. **Eligibility Criteria**
   - Section begins outlining mandatory eligibility parameters and required credentials

nodes.title: Eligibility Criteria
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document outlines the technical and eligibility evaluation framework for an e-tender process for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop, deploy, operate, and monetize AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services.

## Main Points Covered:

### 1. **Bid Evaluation Process (Three-Stage)**
- **Stage 1**: Verification of minimum eligibility criteria and technical scoring (minimum 70 marks required on QCBS basis)
- **Stage 2**: Financial bid opening for qualified bidders
- **Stage 3**: Selection of H1 bidder based on combined technical and financial evaluation using QCBS methodology

### 2. **Eligibility Criteria (10 Parameters)**
The document details mandatory requirements including:
- **Company Existence**: Must be registered under Indian laws, operating for 5 years (relaxed to 1.5 years for MSEs/Startups)
- **Financial Turnover**: Minimum Rs 5 Cr average annual turnover in chatbot services (Rs 1.30 Cr for MSEs/Startups)
- **Relevant Project Experience**: Must have developed AI-driven chatbot handling minimum 1 crore user queries annually
- **Monetization Experience**: Revenue generation through digital ads (Rs 1 Cr annually or two works of Rs 80 lakhs each; relaxed for MSEs/Startups)
- **Language Capability**: Experience with at least three Indian languages
- **Certifications**: ISO 27001:2015 required
- **No Blacklisting**: Clean record with government/PSU organizations
- **Cloud Storage**: Deployment on MeitY-empaneled Cloud Service Provider
- **Technical Support**: Dedicated support team commitment

### 3. **Documentation Requirements**
Specific credentials required for each eligibility parameter, including certificates, work orders, client letters, CA-certified statements with mandatory UDIN numbers, and undertakings.

### 4. **Technical Scoring Criteria**
- Minimum qualifying score: 70 out of 100 marks
- Technical evaluation based on project experience with AI-driven chatbot solutions
- Emphasis on GenAI/LLM or Agentic AI projects in production environments

### 5. **Key Provisions**
- Verification rights reserved by IRCTC through forensic audit
- Penalties for non-compliance: EMD forfeiture and 3-year debarment
- Clarification process during evaluation without price changes
- Special relaxations for MSEs and Startups across multiple criteria

nodes.title: Technical Scoring Criteria
nodes.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This partial document outlines the technical evaluation criteria and scoring methodology for an e-tender related to selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing and deploying an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services. 

**Main Points Covered:**

1. **Penalties and Debarment**: Successful bidders face a 3-year debarment from future IRCTC projects under certain circumstances, and CA Certificates must include mandatory UDIN numbers.

2. **Technical Scoring Requirements**: 
   - Only bidders meeting minimum eligibility criteria will be technically evaluated
   - Minimum qualifying score is 70 out of 100 marks
   - Bids scoring below 70 marks will be disqualified
   - Technical bids receive an absolute score 'T(s)' out of 100 marks

3. **Bid Submission Guidelines**:
   - Financial quotes mentioned in technical bids may result in rejection
   - IRCTC reserves the right to request additional verification information
   - Bidders must notify IRCTC of any status changes affecting eligibility

4. **Technical Evaluation Criteria (QCBS)**:
   - **Project Experience** carries 20 marks maximum
   - Bidders must demonstrate experience in developing, deploying, and operating AI-driven chatbot solutions (GenAI/LLM or Agentic AI)
   - Requirements include: minimum three languages support, 1000+ average daily users, and application in ticketing/travel/customer service platforms
   - Scoring: More than 3 projects = 10 marks; 1-3 projects = 5 marks
   - Required documentation: Client certificates and live demonstrations

nodes.title: Technical Evaluation (Technical Score - QCBS)
nodes.summary:
This partial document outlines the technical evaluation and scoring criteria for an e-tender to select a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing and deploying an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **Mandatory Requirements**: CA certificates must include UDIN numbers; successful bidders face 3-year debarment for non-compliance.

2. **Technical Scoring Threshold**: Bidders must score minimum 70 out of 100 marks in technical evaluation to qualify for financial bid opening.

3. **Technical Evaluation Criteria (100 marks total)**:
   - **Project Experience (20 marks)**: Experience with AI-driven chatbot solutions in ticketing/travel/customer service with multilingual support and handling minimum 1 crore queries annually
   - **Multilingual Automation Capability (30 marks)**: Demonstrated experience with Indian languages beyond Hindi and English using AI/ML technology
   - **Technical Presentation (50 marks)**: Live demonstration of Gen AI/Agentic AI technologies, including architecture, UI/UX, intent management, and end-to-end transaction processing

4. **Tender Evaluation Process**: Three-stage QCBS (Quality and Cost-Based Selection) methodology:
   - Stage 1: Eligibility verification and technical scoring (minimum 70 marks required)
   - Stage 2: Financial bid opening for qualified bidders
   - Stage 3: Combined evaluation using formula: Total Score = 0.7 × T(s) + 0.3 × F(s)

5. **Selection Criteria**: Highest composite score wins; technical score used as tiebreaker.

nodes.title: Tender Evaluation Criteria
nodes.summary:
This partial document describes the evaluation and selection process for a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC Services. The main points covered include:

1. **Technical Evaluation Scoring**: Bidders must score at least 70% marks in technical criteria to qualify for financial bid evaluation.

2. **Tender Evaluation Methodology (QCBS - Quality and Cost Based Selection)**: 
   - 70% weightage to technical marks
   - 30% weightage to financial quote
   - Three-stage evaluation process

3. **Stage-wise Evaluation Process**:
   - Stage 1: Eligibility qualification and technical scoring (minimum 70 marks required)
   - Stage 2: Financial bid opening for qualified bidders only
   - Stage 3: Combined QCBS scoring using formula: Total Score = 0.7 x T(s) + 0.3 x F(s)

4. **Scoring Calculations**: Detailed formulas and examples for:
   - Technical Score (T(s)) normalization
   - Financial Score (F(s)) based on Total Minimum Business Commitment (TMBC)
   - Combined QCBS scoring with worked examples

5. **Award of Contract**: Contract awarded to bidder with highest total score (H1); tie-breaker based on higher technical score.

6. **Compliance Requirements**:
   - Integrity Pact signing requirement
   - Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) within 30 days of work order acceptance

7. **Payment Terms**: Quarterly advance payment structure for Total Minimum Business Commitment value to IRCTC, with first payment due within 30 days of LOA acceptance.

nodes.title: Award of Contract
nodes.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This partial document outlines the final stages of a tender process for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop and operate an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **Award of Contract Process**: The contract will be awarded to the bidder with the highest total score (H1) based on the QCBS (Quality and Cost-Based Selection) method. In case of a tie, technical scores will be the deciding factor. IRCTC reserves the right to reject bids at any stage.

2. **Integrity Pact Requirements**: Bidders must sign an Integrity Pact (Annexure-VII) committing both parties to avoid corrupt practices throughout the contract process. This signed pact is part of the eligibility criteria.

3. **Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)**: The successful bidder must sign an NDA (Annexure-VIII) within 30 days of work order acceptance.

4. **Financial Bid Evaluation**: Only technically qualified bidders scoring more than 70% in QCBS standards will have their financial bids opened. Financial bids must be submitted online using the specified format (Annexure-II).

5. **Payment Terms**: The document details the Total Minimum Business Commitment (TMBC) payment structure, requiring:
   - Advance quarterly payments (every 3 months)
   - First quarterly payment within 30 days of LOA acceptance
   - An example calculation for a ₹9 crore TMBC over 3 years

nodes.title: NDA - Non Disclosure Agreement
nodes.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This partial document outlines key procurement and contractual terms for an e-tender related to selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing and deploying AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **Award of Contract Process**: The contract will be awarded to the bidder with the highest total score (H1) based on a specified selection method. In case of tied scores, technical scores serve as the tiebreaker. IRCTC reserves the right to accept, reject, or annul bids without liability.

2. **Integrity Pact Requirements**: Bidders must sign an Integrity Pact (Annexure-VII) committing both parties to avoid corrupt practices throughout the contract process. This signed pact is part of the eligibility criteria.

3. **Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)**: The successful bidder must execute an NDA with IRCTC within 30 days of work order acceptance, as per the format in Annexure-VIII.

4. **Financial Bid Evaluation**: Only technically qualified bidders scoring above 70% in QCBS (Quality and Cost-Based Selection) standards will have their financial bids opened. Financial bids must be submitted online using the prescribed format (Annexure-II) with no deviations allowed.

5. **Payment Terms**: The document specifies that the Total Minimum Business Commitment (TMBC) value must be paid to IRCTC on a quarterly advance basis, with the first quarterly payment due within 30 days of LOA (Letter of Acceptance) issuance. An example illustrates the payment structure for a ₹9 crore commitment over 3 years.

structure.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This section of the tender document outlines key eligibility and evaluation criteria for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop and operate an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **MSME/Start-up Consortium Restrictions**: MSMEs and start-ups that receive relaxation on turnover criteria are prohibited from forming consortiums, making consortium provisions inapplicable to them.

2. **Conflict of Interest Provisions**: Comprehensive guidelines defining conflicts of interest that would disqualify bidders, including:
   - Common ownership or controlling shareholders between bidders
   - Shared constituents among different bidders
   - Financial relationships (subsidies, grants, loans) between bidders
   - Same legal representation for multiple bidders
   - Relationships that could enable information sharing or bid influence
   - Prior consultant involvement in project preparation

3. **Penalties for Conflicts**: IRCTC reserves the right to forfeit bid security and/or performance security if conflicts of interest are identified, with IRCTC having sole discretion in determining such conflicts.

4. **Bid Evaluation System**: The tender follows a Quality cum Cost Based Selection (QCBS) methodology using a Two Packet System for bid evaluation.

structure.title: PAYMENT TERMS
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document outlines key contractual and financial terms for an e-tender to select a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing and operating AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

## Award and Integrity Requirements
- Contract awarded to bidder with highest total score (H1) using QCBS method
- In case of tie, higher technical score prevails
- Mandatory Integrity Pact and Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) requirements

## Financial Bid Criteria
- Only technically qualified bidders scoring >70% in QCBS standards will have financial bids opened
- Financial bids must follow prescribed format with no deviations

## Payment Structure
- **Total Minimum Business Commitment (TMBC)**: Paid quarterly in advance over 3-year period
- **Security Deposit**: 5% of Annual Minimum Business Commitment
- **Revenue Sharing**: 70% to IRCTC, 30% to bidder
- **Service Fee**: ₹10 per chatbot/WhatsApp bot-initiated ticket booking
- Late payment penalty: 12% per annum interest

## Implementation Timeline
- Go-Live deadline: 3 months from LOA issuance across all IRCTC platforms
- Penalties for delays: 5% per annum interest or adjustment from security deposit
- Revenue during partial implementation: 70:30 split

## Contract Terms
- Initial period: 3 years with 1+1 year extension option
- Extension terms: 10% increment on Annual Minimum Business Commitment
- IRCTC invoicing based on higher of quarterly minimum commitment or actual revenue

## Service Level Agreement (SLA)
- System uptime: ≥99.5% per month (penalty: ₹10,000 per 0.1% shortfall)
- Bot reply time: ≤3 seconds (penalty: ₹2,000 per non-compliance instance)
- IRCTC reserves rights to terminate contract for payment defaults or service failures

structure.title: SLA & PENALTY CLAUSE
structure.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This partial document outlines key commercial, operational, and legal terms for an e-tender to select a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing and operating AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **Revenue Sharing Model**: 70:30 split between IRCTC and the bidder, with IRCTC receiving 70% of revenues from ticket bookings initiated through the chatbot/WhatsApp bot.

2. **Service Fees**: An additional ₹10 fee per chatbot/WhatsApp bot-initiated ticket booking.

3. **Minimum Business Commitment**: Quarterly minimum business targets must be met by the bidder, with IRCTC invoicing for shortfalls or actual higher revenue achieved.

4. **Payment Terms**: Payment due within 15 days of invoice, with 12% per annum interest on delays and potential service cancellation for frequent defaults.

5. **Service Level Agreement (SLA) Parameters**: Detailed performance metrics including:
   - System uptime (≥99.5% monthly)
   - Bot reply time (≤3 seconds)
   - Booking success rate (≥95%)
   - Language understanding rate (≥90%)
   - Restrictions on ad categories
   - Data security requirements

6. **Penalty Clauses**: Specific financial penalties for non-compliance with each SLA parameter.

7. **Delivery Details**: Consignee and address information for the project.

8. **Intellectual Property Rights**: All IP developed under the project, including software, data, WhatsApp assets, and documentation, belongs exclusively to IRCTC.

structure.title: CONSIGNEE & DELIVERY ADDRESS
structure.summary:
This partial document outlines key performance metrics, penalties, delivery details, and intellectual property rights for an e-tender concerning AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **Performance Metrics and Penalties**: Four key performance indicators with specific thresholds and associated penalties:
   - Booking Success Rate (≥95%) with ₹5,000 penalty per 1% drop monthly
   - Language Understanding Rate (≥90%) with ₹5,000 monthly penalty if accuracy drops in two or more languages
   - Restrictions on serving certain ad categories with ₹10,000 penalty per incident and potential chatbot disablement
   - Data breach/security incidents requiring bidder to compensate direct financial losses

2. **Consignee and Delivery Information**: Specifies the delivery address as Group General Manager/IT-1 at IRCTC's Internet Ticketing Center, World Trade Centre, New Delhi.

3. **Intellectual Property Rights (IPR)**: Establishes that all intellectual property developed exclusively for IRCTC under this project—including e-ticketing applications, software code, dashboards, data, documentation, WhatsApp number, and WhatsApp Business API Profile—shall be the sole and exclusive property of IRCTC.

structure.title: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS (IPR)

nodes.title: Ownership of Intellectual Property
nodes.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This partial document outlines key contractual terms for an e-tender related to selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing and operating an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **Performance Metrics and Penalties**: Establishes specific Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) including:
   - Booking Success Rate (≥95%)
   - Language Understanding Rate (≥90%)
   - Penalties ranging from ₹5,000 to ₹10,000 for various performance failures
   - Provisions for data breach incidents and restricted category advertisements

2. **Delivery Details**: Specifies the consignee as Group General Manager/IT-1 at IRCTC's Internet Ticketing Center in New Delhi

3. **Intellectual Property Rights (IPR)**: Comprehensive provisions covering:
   - IRCTC's exclusive ownership of all developed intellectual property
   - Restrictions on bidder's use of developed solutions
   - Third-party IP licensing requirements
   - Source code escrow arrangements
   - IP transfer obligations upon contract completion or termination
   - 3-month knowledge transfer commitment post-contract

4. **Contract Termination Clauses**: Details conditions for contract termination including:
   - 15-day remedy notice for defaults
   - Three-month notice period for serious/repeated defaults
   - Events of default (non-payment, negligence, acts against IRCTC interests)
   - Security deposit forfeiture provisions

nodes.title: Restrictions on Use
nodes.summary:
This partial document covers the following main points:

**Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) Management:**
- IRCTC retains ownership of all intellectual property developed during the contract
- Restrictions on bidder's use, replication, distribution, or commercialization of developed IP without IRCTC's written consent
- Requirements for third-party software licensing and indemnification
- Source code escrow arrangements with regular audits at bidder's cost
- Complete IP transfer obligations upon contract completion or termination, including source codes, credentials, and technical documentation
- Knowledge transfer (KT) requirements with 3-month post-contract support commitment

**Contract Termination Provisions:**
- Default remediation process with 15-day notice period
- Consequences including security deposit forfeiture and quarterly advance payment forfeiture
- Three-month notice requirement for termination due to serious and repeated defaults
- Specific events of default including:
  - Non-payment of IRCTC revenue
  - Willful negligence in responding to IRCTC
  - Acts against IRCTC's interests
- Financial settlement obligations upon contract expiry or termination

The document is part of an e-tender for selecting a Technology Service Provider for AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services.

structure.title: TERMINATION OF CONTRACT
structure.summary:
This partial document describes contractual terms for an AI-based chatbot solution tender by IRCTC, covering the following main points:

**Intellectual Property Rights (Section 13):**
- All IP developed during contract execution belongs to IRCTC
- Restrictions on bidder's use, replication, or commercialization without IRCTC consent
- Requirements for third-party IP licensing and indemnification
- Source code escrow arrangements and IP transfer obligations upon contract completion/termination
- 3-month knowledge transfer commitment post-contract

**Contract Termination (Section 14):**
- 15-day notice period for breach remedy before termination
- IRCTC's right to terminate with 3 months' notice for serious/repeated defaults
- Events of default including: non-payment of revenue, willful negligence, acts against IRCTC interest, payment defaults, and unauthorized data transfers
- Security deposit forfeiture provisions

**Exit Clause (Section 15):**
- IRCTC's discretionary right to terminate without notice
- Termination provisions for regulatory compliance issues
- Pro-rata refund of security deposit and advance payment after deducting dues
- Bidder's option to exit with 90 days' notice

**Service Agreement (Section 16):**
- Requirement for formal service agreement upon purchase order issuance

**Default by Successful Bidder (Section 17):**
- Critical nature of the project emphasized
- Serious defaults include: withdrawal after award, mid-contract abandonment, non-delivery, false information, and unauthorized credential/data sharing

structure.title: EXIT CLAUSE
structure.summary:
This partial document section covers the termination, exit, and default provisions for an IRCTC e-tender regarding AI-based chatbot solutions. The main points include:

1. **Termination Grounds**: Conditions for contract termination including repeated payment defaults and unauthorized data transfer to third parties.

2. **Exit Clause**: 
   - IRCTC's right to terminate or suspend services at any time without prior notice or reason, including for regulatory compliance
   - Bidder's option to exit with 90 days' prior notice
   - Provisions for refunding security deposits and advance payments on pro-rata basis after deducting pending dues

3. **Service Agreement**: Requirement for successful bidder to enter into a formal service agreement upon receiving purchase order/award of contract.

4. **Default Conditions**: Critical defaults by successful bidder including:
   - Withdrawal after receiving purchase order
   - Abandoning contract during implementation or support period
   - Non-delivery of project
   - Providing false information
   - Sharing IRCTC credentials with third parties
   - Unauthorized data transfers

The document emphasizes the critical nature of the project and IRCTC's expectations for bidder commitment and data security.

structure.title: DEFAULT BY SUCCESSFUL BIDDER
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document covers the following main points from an e-tender for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for AI-based Chatbot Solutions for IRCTC Services:

## Key Sections Covered:

1. **Termination Grounds (Section 14.3.4-14.3.5)**
   - Repeated payment defaults during contract period
   - Unauthorized data transfer to third parties

2. **Exit Clause (Section 15)**
   - IRCTC's right to terminate or suspend services at any time without prior notice
   - Provisions for refund of Security Deposit and Advance Payment on pro-rata basis
   - Bidder's option to exit with 90 days' prior notice
   - Deduction of pending dues from refunds

3. **Service Agreement (Section 16)**
   - Requirement for successful bidder to enter into Service Agreement upon Purchase Order issuance

4. **Default by Successful Bidder (Section 17)**
   - Definition of serious defaults including:
     - Withdrawal after receiving purchase order
     - Abandoning contract during implementation
     - Non-delivery of project
     - Providing wrong information
     - Sharing IRCTC credentials or unauthorized data transfer
   - Consequences of default: confiscation of advance payment, payment stoppage, 3-year debarment, contract termination

5. **Arbitration (Section 18)**
   - Dispute resolution procedure
   - Amicable settlement as first step
   - Arbitration process under Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996
   - Sole Arbitrator appointment from IRCTC's empaneled arbitrators
   - Equal sharing of arbitration costs
   - Continuation of obligations during dispute resolution

structure.title: ARBITRATION
structure.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This partial document outlines key contractual terms and conditions for an e-tender related to selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing and deploying AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **Default and Penalties**: Conditions under which a bidder is treated as a 'defaulter' and IRCTC's rights to take actions including confiscating advance payments, withholding payments, debarring the defaulter for 3 years, or terminating the contract.

2. **Arbitration and Dispute Resolution**: A two-tier dispute resolution mechanism consisting of:
   - Initial amicable settlement between parties
   - Arbitration through a sole arbitrator from IRCTC's empaneled list under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996
   - Provisions for continuing performance during disputes and sharing arbitration costs equally

3. **Force Majeure**: Clauses protecting both parties from liability for non-performance due to events beyond their control (war, natural disasters, epidemics, strikes, etc.), with requirements for notice within seven days of occurrence.

4. **Information Security, Compliance and Audit Requirements**: Security obligations including:
   - Prohibition on sharing IRCTC data with third parties without approval
   - Requirements for preventing unauthorized access
   - Return of all information upon contract termination
   - Confidentiality and protection of IRCTC's proprietary information and business data

structure.title: FORCE MAJEURE
structure.summary:
This partial document describes key contractual clauses from an e-tender for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop and operate an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **Dispute Settlement Continuation**: Obligations that contract performance must continue during dispute resolution, with provisions binding on successors and assigns, including cases of premature termination.

2. **Force Majeure Clause**: Details circumstances beyond parties' control (war, natural disasters, epidemics, strikes, etc.) that excuse non-performance, requiring seven-day notice of such events, with no termination rights or damage claims during force majeure events.

3. **Information Security Requirements**: Comprehensive data protection obligations including:
   - Prohibition on sharing IRCTC data with third parties without approval
   - Mandatory security measures to prevent unauthorized access
   - Requirements to return all IRCTC information upon contract termination
   - Confidentiality obligations regarding IRCTC's proprietary information and business data
   - Duty to safeguard proprietary information with reasonable care

4. **Compliance and Audit Requirements**: Reference to information security, compliance and audit framework (though details appear in subsequent sections).

structure.title: INFORMATION SECURITY, COMPLIANCE AND AUDIT REQUIREMENTS
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document covers key contractual and compliance provisions for an e-tender related to the selection of a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing and deploying an AI-based chatbot solution (AskDisha) for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

## Main Points:

1. **Force Majeure Clause (Section 19)**: Outlines conditions under which neither party is liable for non-performance due to events beyond their control (war, natural disasters, epidemics, strikes, etc.), with requirements for notice within seven days of occurrence.

2. **Information Security Requirements (Section 21.1)**: 
   - Prohibition on sharing IRCTC data with third parties without authorization
   - Security measures to prevent unauthorized access
   - Return of all information upon contract termination
   - Protection of IRCTC's proprietary information and business data

3. **Confidentiality Requirements (Section 21.2)**:
   - Restrictions on use of confidential information and IRCTC branding
   - Non-disclosure obligations for all tender-related information
   - Requirement for signing a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)
   - Bidder responsibility for NDA breaches by personnel or sub-contractors

4. **Statutory Compliance (Section 21.3)**: Requirements for compliance with all applicable central, state, and local laws, including labor laws, with indemnification of IRCTC against any violations.

5. **Annexure I - Cover Letter/Application Form**: Template for bidder's formal submission, including commitment to execute the three-year chatbot project, acceptance of terms and conditions, and validity period of 120 days for the offer.

structure.title: BID DOCUMENT PART -II: ANNEXURES OF THE TENDER
structure.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This partial document is a bid submission template for an e-tender issued by IRCTC (Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation) for selecting a Technology Service Provider. The main points covered include:

1. **Project Scope**: Development, deployment, operation, upgrading, and ad monetization of an AI-based chatbot solution called "AskDisha" for IRCTC services on Indian Railway websites and mobile applications.

2. **Contract Duration**: Three (3) years service period.

3. **Bid Format Requirements**: The document provides Annexure I, which is a cover letter/RFP application form template that bidders must submit on their company letterhead.

4. **Bidder Commitments**: The template requires bidders to:
   - Acknowledge understanding of all tender conditions
   - Commit to executing the work as per quoted financial bid
   - Agree to abide by scope of work, terms and conditions, SOR (Schedule of Rates), and general terms
   - Keep the offer valid for 120 days from tender opening date
   - Accept that purchase order issuance constitutes a binding contract until formal agreement execution

5. **Addressee**: The bid is to be submitted to GGM/IT at IRCTC Corporate Office, New Delhi.

6. **Reference Number**: E-Tender No. 2025/IRCTC/CO/SER/Chatbot ASKDISHA

structure.title: Annexure: I – Cover Letter /RFP Application Form
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains the annexures section (Part II) of an e-tender issued by IRCTC (Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation) for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop, deploy, operate, upgrade, and monetize an AI-based chatbot solution called "AskDisha."

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Annexure I - Cover Letter/RFP Application Form:**
   - Template for bidders to submit their proposal on company letterhead
   - Reference to tender number: 2025/IRCTC/CO/SER/Chatbot ASKDISHA
   - Commitment to execute chatbot development for IRCTC and Indian Railway websites/mobile apps for 3 years
   - Agreement to keep offer valid for 120 days from tender opening
   - Detailed bidder information requirements including:
     - Company details and contact information
     - CEO and authorized signatory details
     - MSE/Startup category declaration (if applicable)

2. **Annexure II - Schedule of Rate (SOR):**
   - Financial bid template for Total Minimum Business Commitment (TMBC)
   - TMBC to be quoted for 3-year period (excluding GST)
   - Key conditions:
     - Initial contract period: 3 years with possible 1+1 year extension
     - Security deposit: 5% of Annual TMBC value
     - GST applicable over and above IRCTC share
     - Amount to be quoted in both figures and words (words prevail in case of discrepancy)
     - Values allowed up to two decimal places only

structure.title: Annexure: II –Schedule of Rate (SOR)*
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains key annexures from an e-tender issued by Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation (IRCTC) for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop, deploy, operate, and monetize an AI-based chatbot solution called ASKDISHA.

## Main Points Covered:

**Annexure II - Schedule of Rate (SOR):**
- Financial bid template for the tender (Tender No. 2025/IRCTC/CO/SER/Chatbot ASKDISHA)
- Requires bidders to submit Total Minimum Business Commitment (TMBC) for a 3-year period
- Key conditions include:
  - TMBC amount to be quoted excluding GST
  - Initial contract period of 3 years with possible extension of 1+1 year
  - Security deposit requirement of 5% of Annual TMBC value
  - GST to be charged over and above IRCTC share
  - Specific formatting requirements (positive numbers, up to two decimal places, amounts in Rupees)
  - Discrepancy resolution favoring amounts written in words

**Annexure III - List of Blocked Category Ads:**
- Undertaking template for bidders to comply with advertising restrictions
- Lists 13 blocked advertising categories including:
  - Discrimination-based content
  - Adult/sexual content and pornography
  - Gambling, astrology, and get-rich-quick schemes
  - Political and religious content
  - Tobacco and intoxicant advertisements
  - Content harmful to national sentiments or security
  - Weight loss supplements, bitcoins, and other restricted categories
- Requires authorized signatory commitment to maintain these restrictions

structure.title: Annexure III - List of Blocked Category Ads
structure.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This partial document contains annexures from an e-tender for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop and operate an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services. The main points covered include:

1. **Annexure III - Blocked Category Ads Undertaking:** A formal declaration template requiring bidders to commit to maintaining a list of prohibited advertisement categories. The blocked categories include:
   - Discrimination-based content (gender, religion, race, caste, nationality, disability, sexual orientation)
   - Adult content (pornography, nudity, sexual health, lingerie, dating)
   - Gambling, casino, black magic, astrology, and get-rich-quick schemes
   - Drugs and supplements
   - Political and religious content
   - Weight gain/loss supplements
   - Ringtones and downloadables
   - Cosmetic procedures and body modification
   - Cryptocurrencies (Bitcoins)
   - Socially offensive content
   - Tobacco and intoxicant advertisements
   - Content harmful to national sentiments, religious communities, or national security

2. **Annexure IV - Audit Annual Report:** A declaration template for bidders to provide their annual turnover from digital advertisements on chatbot platforms for financial years 2022-23, 2023-24, and 2024-25, supported by GST returns and CA reports. This covers online advertising revenue from chatbot solutions on websites, mobile apps, and mobile web platforms.

structure.title: ANNEXURE – IV –Audit Annual Report
structure.summary:
**Description of Partial Document:**

This partial document contains two key annexures from an e-tender for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop and deploy an AI-based chatbot solution for IRCTC services:

**Main Points Covered:**

1. **Annexure IV - Audit Annual Report:**
   - Requires bidders to submit audited annual reports or Chartered Accountant reports
   - Focuses on annual turnover specifically from digital advertisement on chatbot platforms
   - Covers three financial years: 2022-2023, 2023-2024, and 2024-2025
   - Turnover must be based on GST returns
   - Includes all forms of online advertising on chatbot solutions across websites, mobile apps, and mobile web

2. **Annexure V - Undertaking for Cloud and Data Storage:**
   - Requires bidders to confirm deployment of IRCTC chatbot on their infrastructure/cloud
   - Mandates use of India-based cloud/physical data centers only
   - Requires cloud solution to be MEITY (Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology) compliant
   - Includes warning about consequences of false information (tender/contract rejection, forfeiture of EMD/SD, and pecuniary action)

Both annexures require authorized signatory declarations with company seal, date, and place.

structure.title: ANNEXURE – V- UNDERTAKING FOR CLOUD AND DATA STORAGE
structure.summary:
This partial document contains two key annexures from an e-tender for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services:

**Main Points Covered:**

1. **Annexure V - Undertaking for Cloud and Data Storage:**
   - Requires bidders to provide a formal undertaking on company letterhead
   - Confirms deployment of IRCTC chatbot on bidder's infrastructure/cloud
   - Mandates use of India-based cloud/physical data centers only
   - Requires MEITY (Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology) compliant cloud solutions
   - Warns of consequences for false information including tender/contract rejection, forfeiture of EMD/SD, and pecuniary action
   - Requires authorized signatory declaration with CA report enclosure

2. **Annexure VI - Declaration on Blacklisting/Debarment:**
   - Requires bidders to declare non-blacklisting status
   - Confirms bidder has not been blacklisted by any PSU/Government organization in the last three years
   - Confirms no appearance on negative/black lists for breach of laws, regulatory violations, or agreement breaches
   - Declares no default status with any bank/financial institution
   - Requires authorized signatory details including name, designation, and date

Both annexures are mandatory compliance documents for bidders participating in the e-tender process for IRCTC's AI chatbot development and ad monetization services.

structure.title: Annexure VI - No fraudulent practices or blacklisting
structure.summary:
This partial document covers the following main points:

**Primary Subject:** E-Tender for Selection of Technology Service Provider (TSP) for AI-based Chatbot Solutions for IRCTC Services

**Main Components:**

1. **Annexure VI - Declaration on Blacklisting/Debarment:**
   - Template for bidders to declare they are not blacklisted by any public sector undertaking or government organization in the last three years
   - Confirmation of no defaults with banks or financial institutions
   - Required to be submitted on bidder's letterhead with authorized signatory details

2. **Annexure VII - Integrity Pact:**
   - Pre-contract agreement between IRCTC and bidders
   - Establishes commitment to prevent corruption and maintain transparency
   - Defines it as a preliminary qualification requirement for bidders
   - **Section 1 - IRCTC's Commitments:**
     - Prevention of corruption measures
     - Prohibition of employees demanding or accepting illegal benefits
     - Equal treatment of all bidders during tender process
     - Non-disclosure of confidential/additional information to favor any particular bidder

**Purpose:** These annexures serve as mandatory compliance documents ensuring ethical business practices, transparency, and fair competition in the tender process for IRCTC's AI-based chatbot solution development and deployment project.

structure.title: ANNEXURE – VII- INTEGRITY PACT
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document covers the **Integrity Pact and Confidentiality Agreement** sections (Annexures VII and VIII) of an e-tender for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) for developing AI-based chatbot solutions for IRCTC services.

## Main Points Covered:

### Annexure VII - Integrity Pact:
1. **Pre-Contract Agreement**: A bilateral agreement between IRCTC and bidders/contractors to prevent corruption and ensure transparency
2. **IRCTC Commitments**: Pledges to prevent corruption, treat all bidders equally, provide same information to all parties, and exclude prejudiced persons
3. **Bidder/Contractor Commitments**: Obligations including:
   - No bribery or illegal benefits to IRCTC employees
   - No cartelization or anti-competitive agreements
   - Disclosure of agents, intermediaries, and payments
   - Disclosure of previous transgressions with government organizations (last 3 years)
4. **Violation Consequences**: Disqualification, exclusion from future dealings, and financial penalties (EMD/Performance Bank Guarantee forfeiture)
5. **Independent External Monitors (IEMs)**: Two appointed monitors to review compliance and handle complaints objectively
6. **Pact Duration**: Expires 12 months after last payment for contractors, 6 months after contract award for other bidders
7. **Dispute Resolution**: Optional mediation through IEM panel before legal action

### Annexure VIII - Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA):
1. **Confidentiality Requirements**: Protection of technical and non-technical information, trade secrets, network details, and proprietary data
2. **Recipient Obligations**: Maintain confidentiality and ensure employees/agents/contractors do the same
3. **Limitations on Obligations**: Exceptions for publicly known information or information already in recipient's possession

structure.title: Annexure VIII - NDA
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document covers the following main points from an IRCTC e-tender for AI-based chatbot solutions:

## 1. **Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) Requirements**
- Must be executed on non-judicial stamp paper of Rs.100 within 15 days of purchase order award
- Establishes confidentiality obligations between IRCTC and the selected Technology Service Provider (TSP)
- Defines confidential information including technical data, network infrastructure, software programs, customer information, and proprietary details
- Outlines recipient obligations for handling, protecting, and restricting disclosure of confidential information
- Specifies exceptions where confidentiality obligations do not apply (publicly known information, independently developed information, legally required disclosures)

## 2. **Key Contractual Terms**
- **Intellectual Property Rights**: All confidential information remains IRCTC's property
- **Duration**: Confidentiality obligations extend for five years after contract termination
- **Authorized Purpose**: Use of information limited to project scope (mentioned as "Push Notification on IRCTC Owned websites and Apps" and chatbot development)
- **Return of Documents**: Requirement to return or destroy all confidential materials upon termination
- **Remedies**: Provisions for injunctive relief and indemnification for breaches causing irreparable harm
- **Governing Law**: Indian law with exclusive jurisdiction in Delhi courts

## 3. **Glossary of Technical Terms**
Defines key terminology used throughout the tender including:
- **Agentic AI**: Proactive AI capable of goal-oriented task execution
- **AI-Powered Chatbot**: Advanced conversational system using Generative and Agentic AI
- **AskDisha**: IRCTC's existing AI chatbot platform
- **Bidder and Consortium**: Definitions of participating entities
- **DPDP Act**: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 compliance requirements
- **EMD**: Earnest Money Deposit of ₹5,00,000
- **Financial Bid**: Total Minimum Business Commitment (TMBC) quotation structure

The document emphasizes data protection, confidentiality, legal compliance, and establishes the framework for vendor obligations in developing and operating IRCTC's AI-based chatbot solution.

structure.title: ANNEXURE – IX- GLOSSARY
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains the **Glossary (Annexure IX)** and **Document Checklist (Annexure X)** sections from an IRCTC e-tender for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop and operate AI-based chatbot solutions.

## Main Points Covered:

### Glossary Section (Annexure IX):
- **Definitions of key technical terms** including:
  - AI technologies: Agentic AI, Generative AI, AI-Powered Chatbot
  - AskDisha (IRCTC's chatbot name)
  - Tender-related terms: Bidder, Consortium, Lead Member, TSP
  - Financial terms: EMD, TMBC, Revenue Sharing (70:30 split), Security Deposit, RDS
  - Compliance terms: DPDP Act, MEITY guidelines, IPR, NDA
  - Evaluation method: QCBS (Quality cum Cost Based Selection - 70% technical, 30% financial)
  - Project milestones: LOA, Go-Live (within 3 months)
  - Platform components: WhatsApp Bot, Indian Railways Website/App integration
  - Special categories: MSE/Startup with relaxed criteria

### Document Checklist Section (Annexure X):
- **Mandatory documents required from bidders**, including:
  1. Cover letter/application form
  2. Audit annual reports
  3. Various undertakings and compliance certificates
  4. Incorporation and registration documents (GST, PAN)
  5. Consortium agreement (if applicable)
  6. 5-year business operation proof (relaxed to 1.5 years for MSE/Startups)
  7. Authorization documents (PoA or Board Resolution)
  8. Financial statements for FY 2022-23, 2023-24, and CA certificate for 2024-25 with mandatory UDIN number

structure.title: ANNEXURE – X: Document Checklist
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is an annexure (Annexure-X) from an e-tender document issued by IRCTC for selecting a Technology Service Provider (TSP) to develop, deploy, operate, upgrade, and monetize an AI-based chatbot solution.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Document Checklist Purpose**: Provides a comprehensive list of mandatory documents that bidders must submit as part of their tender application.

2. **Document Categories**: The checklist includes 16 items covering:
   - **Administrative Documents**: Cover letter, application forms, undertakings, and integrity pacts
   - **Legal/Registration Documents**: Certificate of incorporation, GST registration, PAN card, consortium agreements (if applicable)
   - **Financial Documents**: Annual audited financial statements, CA certificates with UDIN numbers for FY 2022-23, 2023-24, and 2024-25
   - **Experience Proof**: Work orders and client letters demonstrating AI chatbot development and deployment experience
   - **Technical Capability**: Evidence of handling at least 1 crore queries annually, revenue generation through digital advertisements
   - **Compliance Certificates**: ISO 27001:2015 certification
   - **Operational Commitments**: Undertaking to provide dedicated support team

3. **Special Provisions**: Relaxed eligibility criteria for MSEs and Startups (1.5 years instead of 5 years of business operation)

4. **Structured Format**: Each item includes reference to specific clauses and annexures for proper documentation guidance
